1	import { ChokidarOptions, watch as createWatcher } from 'chokidar'
2	import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
3	import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
4	import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
5	import glob from 'globby'
6	import path from 'path'
7	
8	import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
9	import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
10	import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
11	import { map } from '../blaze/map'
12	import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
13	import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
14	import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
15	import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
16	import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
17	import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
18	import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'
19	
20	export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
21	export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
22	  name?: string
23	  emitTypes?: boolean
24	  emitMetafile?: boolean
25	  outbase?: never // we don't support this
26	}
27	
28	const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
29	  platform: 'node',
30	  target: 'ES2022',
31	  logLevel: 'error',
32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
33	  metafile: true,
34	} as const
35	
36	/**
37	 * Apply defaults to the original build options
38	 * @param options the original build options
39	 */
40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}

71	export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{

74	  isRequired: boolean
75	  isList: boolean
76	  isUnique: boolean
77	  isId: boolean
78	  isReadOnly: boolean

90	   */
91	  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
92	  dbName?: string | null
93	  hasDefaultValue: boolean

95	  relationFromFields?: string[]

98	  relationOnUpdate?: string
99	  relationName?: string
100	  documentation?: string
101	}>

105	  args: Array<string | number>
106	}>
107	
108	export type FieldDefaultScalar = string | boolean | number
109

112	  type: IndexType

115	  dbName?: string
116	  algorithm?: string
117	  clustered?: boolean

1	import { datamodelEnumToSchemaEnum } from '@prisma/dmmf'
2	
3	import { Enum } from '../Enum'
4	import { GenerateContext } from '../GenerateContext'
5	
6	const jsDocHeader = `/*
7	* This file exports all enum related types from the schema.
8	*
9	* 🟢 You can import this file directly.
10	*/
11	
12	`
13	
14	export function createEnumsFile(context: GenerateContext): string {
15	  const modelEnums: string[] = []
16	  for (const datamodelEnum of context.dmmf.datamodel.enums) {
17	    modelEnums.push(new Enum(datamodelEnumToSchemaEnum(datamodelEnum), false).toTS())
18	  }
19	
20	  // Cannot have a fully empty file with no exports => export empty object
21	  if (modelEnums.length === 0) {
22	    return `${jsDocHeader}
23	
24	// This file is empty because there are no enums in the schema.
25	export {}
26	`
27	  }
28	
29	  return jsDocHeader + modelEnums.join('\n\n')
30	}
31

2	
3	import { GenerateContext } from '../GenerateContext'
4	import { Model } from '../Model'
5	
6	export function createModelFile(context: GenerateContext, modelName: string): string {
7	  const jsDocHeader = `/*
8	 * This file exports the \`${modelName}\` model and its related types.
9	 *
10	 * 🟢 You can import this file directly.
11	 */
12	`
13	
14	  const imports = [
15	    ts.moduleImport(context.runtimeImport).asNamespace('runtime').typeOnly(),
16	    ts.moduleImport(context.importFileName(`../enums`)).asNamespace('$Enums').typeOnly(),
17	    ts.moduleImport(context.importFileName(`../internal/prismaNamespace`)).asNamespace('Prisma').typeOnly(),
18	  ]
19	  const importsString = imports.map((i) => ts.stringify(i)).join('\n')
20	
21	  const model = context.dmmf.typeAndModelMap[modelName]
22	
23	  return jsDocHeader + importsString + '\n' + new Model(model, context).toTS()
24	}

7	import { describe, expect, test } from 'vitest'

10	
11	function addSnapshotPathSanitizer({
12	  test,
13	  get,
14	  set,
15	}: {
16	  test: (value: unknown) => boolean
17	  get: (value: unknown) => string
18	  set: (value: unknown, sanitized: string) => unknown
19	}) {
20	  expect.addSnapshotSerializer({
21	    test: (val) => test(val) && (get(val).includes(__dirname) || get(val).includes('\\')),
22	    serialize(val, config, indentation, depth, refs, printer) {
23	      const newStr = get(val).replaceAll(__dirname, '/project').replaceAll('\\', '/')
24	      return printer(set(val, newStr), config, indentation, depth, refs)
25	    },
26	  })
27	}
28	
29	addSnapshotPathSanitizer({
30	  test: (value: unknown) => typeof value === 'string',
31	  get: (val) => val as string,
32	  set: (_, sanitized) => sanitized,
33	})
34	
35	addSnapshotPathSanitizer({
36	  test: (value: unknown) => value instanceof Error,
37	  get: (val) => (val as Error).message,
38	  set: (val, sanitized) => {
39	    const error = val as Error
40	    error.message = sanitized
41	    return error
42	  },
43	})
44	
45	expect.addSnapshotSerializer({
46	  test(val) {
47	    if (typeof val !== 'object' || val == null) {
48	      return false
49	    }
50	    if (!('fromEnvVar' in val && 'native' in val && 'value' in val)) {
51	      return false
52	    }
53	    return val.native === true && val.value !== 'NATIVE_BINARY_TARGET'
54	  },
55	  serialize(val, config, indentation, depth, refs, printer) {
56	    const newVal = { ...val, value: 'NATIVE_BINARY_TARGET' }
57	    return printer(newVal, config, indentation, depth, refs)
58	  },
59	})
60	
61	expect.addSnapshotSerializer({
62	  test: (val) => val instanceof Error && val.message.includes('\x1B'),
63	  serialize(val, config, indentation, depth, refs, printer) {
64	    val.message = stripVTControlCharacters((val as Error).message)
65	    return printer(val, config, indentation, depth, refs)
66	  },
67	})
68	
69	// Non-standard `AggregateError` from `p-map` (different from standard JS AggregateError).
70	// We flatten it into a new error with a single message containing all the error messages
71	// to avoid problems with stack traces in snapshots.
72	expect.addSnapshotSerializer({
73	  test: (val) => val instanceof Error && val.name === 'AggregateError' && typeof val[Symbol.iterator] === 'function',
74	  serialize(val, config, indentation, depth, refs, printer) {
75	    const error = val as Error & Iterable<Error>
76	    const newError = new Error([...error].map((e) => e.message).join('\n'))
77	    return printer(newError, config, indentation, depth, refs)
78	  },
79	})
80

9	const jsDocHeader = `/*
10	 * This file should be your main import to use Prisma. Through it you get access to all the models, enums, and input types.
11	 * If you're looking for something you can import in the client-side of your application, please refer to the \`browser.ts\` file instead.

18	  const imports = [
19	    ts.moduleImport(context.runtimeImport).asNamespace('runtime'),
20	    ts.moduleImport(context.importFileName('./enums')).asNamespace('$Enums'),
21	    ts.moduleImport(context.importFileName('./internal/class')).asNamespace('$Class'),
22	    ts.moduleImport(context.importFileName('./internal/prismaNamespace')).asNamespace('Prisma'),
23	  ].map((i) => ts.stringify(i))
24	
25	  const exports = [
26	    ts.moduleExportFrom(context.importFileName('./enums')).asNamespace('$Enums'),
27	    ts.moduleExportFrom(context.importFileName('./enums')),
28	    ts
29	      .moduleExport(ts.constDeclaration('PrismaClient').setValue(ts.functionCall('$Class.getPrismaClientClass', [])))
30	      .setDocComment(getPrismaClientClassDocComment(context)),
31	    ts.moduleExport(
32	      ts
33	        .typeDeclaration(
34	          'PrismaClient',
35	          ts
36	            .namedType('$Class.PrismaClient')
37	            .addGenericArgument(ts.namedType('LogOpts'))
38	            .addGenericArgument(ts.namedType('OmitOpts'))
39	            .addGenericArgument(ts.namedType('ExtArgs')),
40	        )
41	        .addGenericParameter(
42	          ts.genericParameter('LogOpts').extends(ts.namedType('Prisma.LogLevel')).default(ts.neverType),
43	        )
44	        .addGenericParameter(
45	          ts
46	            .genericParameter('OmitOpts')
47	            .extends(ts.namedType('Prisma.PrismaClientOptions').subKey('omit'))
48	            .default(ts.namedType('Prisma.PrismaClientOptions').subKey('omit')),
49	        )
50	        .addGenericParameter(
51	          ts
52	            .genericParameter('ExtArgs')
53	            .extends(ts.namedType('runtime.Types.Extensions.InternalArgs'))
54	            .default(ts.namedType('runtime.Types.Extensions.DefaultArgs')),
55	        ),
56	    ),
57	  ].map((e) => ts.stringify(e))
58	
59	  return `${jsDocHeader}
60	${buildPreamble(options.edge, options.moduleFormat)}
61	${imports.join('\n')}
62	
63	${exports.join('\n')}
64	export { Prisma }
65	
66	${modelExports(context).join('\n')}

70	function buildPreamble(edge: boolean, moduleFormat: ModuleFormat): string {
71	  if (edge) {

86	`
87	  }

74	export function getInputType(
75	  introspectionType: QueryIntrospectionType,
76	  nullable: boolean,
77	  enums: DbEnumsList,
78	): ts.TypeBuilder {
79	  const inn = getMappingConfig(introspectionType, enums).in
80	
81	  if (!nullable) {
82	    return inn
83	  } else {
84	    return new ts.UnionType(inn).addVariant(ts.nullType)
85	  }
86	}
87	
88	export function getOutputType(
89	  introspectionType: QueryIntrospectionType,
90	  nullable: boolean,
91	  enums: DbEnumsList,
92	): ts.TypeBuilder {
93	  const out = getMappingConfig(introspectionType, enums).out
94	
95	  if (!nullable) {
96	    return out
97	  } else {
98	    return new ts.UnionType(out).addVariant(ts.nullType)
99	  }
100	}
101	
102	function getMappingConfig(
103	  introspectionType: QueryIntrospectionType,
104	  enums: DbEnumsList,
105	): { in: ts.TypeBuilder; out: ts.TypeBuilder } {
106	  const config = typeMappings[introspectionType]
107	
108	  if (!config) {
109	    if (enums.hasEnum(introspectionType)) {
110	      const type = getEnumType(introspectionType)
111	
112	      return { in: type, out: type }
113	    }
114	
115	    throw new Error('Unknown type')
116	  }
117	
118	  if (config instanceof ts.TypeBuilder) {
119	    return { in: config, out: config }
120	  }
121	
122	  return config
123	}
124	
125	function getEnumType(name: string) {
126	  return ts.namedType('$DbEnums').subKey(name)
127	}

tool_use_result.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a new Prisma generator package called `@prisma/generator-rest-client` that generates a typed REST API client from a Prisma schema.

## Background

This is a Prisma ORM monorepo. All packages live under `packages/`. The build system uses esbuild via `helpers/compile/build.ts` and configs from `helpers/compile/configs.ts`. We need to add a new generator package following the existing patterns.

## What we know from exploration

### Generator Protocol
Generators are implemented using `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper`:
```typescript
generatorHandler({
  async onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> { ... },
  onManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest { ... }
})
```

`GeneratorOptions` contains:
- `options.dmmf` - the DMMF.Document with `datamodel.models`, `datamodel.enums`
- `options.generator.output.value` - resolved output directory path
- `options.schemaPath` - path to schema.prisma

### DMMF types (from `@prisma/dmmf`)
```typescript
type Model = { name: string, fields: Field[], documentation?: string }
type Field = {
  name: string, type: string, kind: 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported',
  isRequired: boolean, isList: boolean, isId: boolean, isUnique: boolean,
  hasDefaultValue: boolean, isReadOnly: boolean
}
type DatamodelEnum = { name: string, values: EnumValue[] }
```

### Package structure pattern
Every generator package needs:
```
packages/generator-rest-client/
├── package.json           (name: "@prisma/generator-rest-client", scripts, deps)
├── tsconfig.json          (extends ../../tsconfig.json)
├── tsconfig.build.json    (extends ../../tsconfig.build.regular.json)
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts           (import adapterConfig; void build(adapterConfig))
└── src/
    ├── index.ts           (exports)
    └── generator.ts       (generatorHandler entry point)
```

### Build pattern (for bundled package like generators)
```typescript
// helpers/build.ts
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'
void build(adapterConfig)
```

The `adapterConfig` uses `src/index.ts` as entry and outputs dual CJS/ESM to `dist/`.

### Registration (optional but preferred)
`packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts` registers built-in generators. However, this new generator is an external one that needs to be invoked as `node ./path-to-dist/index.js`, so the generator protocol uses `generatorHandler` (the out-of-process approach), not the `Generator` interface. The user would configure it in their schema.prisma like:
```
generator restClient {
  provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"
  output   = "./generated/rest-client"
}
```

### Package.json key files to look at:
- `/packages/client-generator-ts/package.json` for exact pattern
- Root `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` paths section needs new package added

## Requirements

The generator should:
1. For each Prisma model, generate a TypeScript interface matching its scalar/enum fields
2. Generate a typed REST API client class with CRUD methods per model: `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`
3. Each method should be properly typed with input/output TypeScript types:
   - `findMany(params?: { where?: Partial<Model>, skip?: number, take?: number }): Promise<Model[]>`
   - `findUnique(params: { where: WhereUniqueInput<Model> }): Promise<Model | null>`
   - `create(params: { data: CreateInput<Model> }): Promise<Model>`
   - `update(params: { where: WhereUniqueInput<Model>, data: UpdateInput<Model> }): Promise<Model>`
   - `delete(params: { where: WhereUniqueInput<Model> }): Promise<Model>`
4. The client should be configurable with a base URL
5. Generate an `index.ts` that exports everything
6. Follow the monorepo's coding conventions (kebab-case files, no barrel files unless necessary, no useless comments)

## Task

Design a step-by-step implementation plan with:
1. Complete file list with paths for the new package
2. Key source file contents/structure (not necessarily complete code, but enough to implement)
3. The TypeScript type generation logic (how to map DMMF scalar types to TS types)
4. How to handle relations (skip relation fields in interfaces, generate only scalar/enum fields)
5. How to generate WhereUniqueInput (use @id fields + @unique fields)
6. How to generate CreateInput (required non-auto-generated fields)
7. Which files need modification in existing packages (tsconfig.build.bundle.json, registry, etc.)
8. Build and test verification steps

Be precise about file contents and implementation details.

import { ChokidarOptions, watch as createWatcher } from 'chokidar'
import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
import glob from 'globby'
import path from 'path'

import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
import { map } from '../blaze/map'
import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'

export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
  name?: string
  emitTypes?: boolean
  emitMetafile?: boolean
  outbase?: never // we don't support this
}

const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
  platform: 'node',
  target: 'ES2022',
  logLevel: 'error',
  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
  metafile: true,
} as const

/**
 * Apply defaults to the original build options
 * @param options the original build options
 */
const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
  format: 'cjs',
  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
  }),
  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
  ...options,
  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
  plugins: [
    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
    resolvePathsPlugin,
    fixImportsPlugin,
    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
    onErrorPlugin,
  ],
  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
})

/**
 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
 * @param options the original build options
 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
 */
function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
  return flatten(
    map(options, (options) => [
      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
      () => applyDefaults(options),
      // ... here can go more steps
    ]),
  )
}

220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {

223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224

Now I have confirmation of the critical bundling detail:

**When `bundle: true` (as with `adapterConfig`):**
- `dependencies` and `peerDependencies` → marked as **external** (NOT bundled)
- `devDependencies` → bundled INTO the output

So the Plan agent was correct: for the out-of-process generator to be self-contained, `@prisma/generator-helper` and `@prisma/dmmf` must be in `devDependencies` so they get bundled into `dist/index.js`. If they were in `dependencies`, they'd be marked external and would need to be installed separately by the user.


Now I'm ready to write the final plan with all the key decisions locked in: the dependency placement, the bundling configuration, the tsconfig path alias, and the build helper pattern.

input.content:
# Plan: `@prisma/generator-rest-client`

## Context

Add a new Prisma generator package to the monorepo that generates a typed TypeScript REST API client from a Prisma schema. The generator implements the standard Prisma out-of-process generator protocol (JSON-RPC over stdin/stderr via `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper`). For each model it produces: a TypeScript interface, three input types (`WhereUniqueInput`, `CreateInput`, `UpdateInput`), and a `RestClient` class with `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete` methods that use the native `fetch` API.

---

## Critical Files to Create

```
packages/generator-rest-client/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.build.json
├── helpers/build.ts
└── src/
    ├── index.ts               ← generatorHandler entry point
    ├── generate.ts            ← orchestrates output file writes
    ├── map-scalar-type.ts     ← DMMF scalar type → TS type string
    ├── build-model-interface.ts
    ├── build-input-types.ts
    ├── build-model-client.ts
    └── build-index-file.ts
```

## Critical File to Modify

- `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` — add path alias for the new package

---

## Implementation Steps

### 1. `package.json`

Follow the pattern of `packages/client-generator-ts/package.json`.

**Key constraint (confirmed from `helpers/compile/build.ts` `getProjectExternals`):** When `bundle: true`, everything in `dependencies` and `peerDependencies` is marked **external** (not bundled). Since the generator must be a self-contained executable (`node ./dist/index.js`), `@prisma/generator-helper` and `@prisma/dmmf` go in `devDependencies` so they are bundled into the output.

```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/generator-rest-client",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "Prisma generator that produces a typed REST API client",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": { "types": "./dist/index.d.ts", "default": "./dist/index.js" },
      "import": { "types": "./dist/index.d.mts", "default": "./dist/index.mjs" }
    }
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/generator-rest-client"
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "dependencies": {},
  "devDependencies": {
    "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator-helper": "workspace:*"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

### 2. `tsconfig.json`

```json
{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.json" }
```

### 3. `tsconfig.build.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "declaration" },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

### 4. `helpers/build.ts`

```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(adapterConfig)
```

`adapterConfig` (from `helpers/compile/configs.ts`) reads `src/index.ts` and emits dual CJS/ESM bundles to `dist/`.

### 5. `src/map-scalar-type.ts`

Maps DMMF scalar type strings to TypeScript types. All types are idiomatic native TS — no Prisma runtime dependencies in the generated client.

```typescript
const SCALAR_TYPE_MAP: Record<string, string> = {
  String: 'string',
  Int: 'number',
  Float: 'number',
  Boolean: 'boolean',
  BigInt: 'bigint',
  DateTime: 'Date',
  Json: 'unknown',
  Bytes: 'string',    // base64 over REST
  Decimal: 'number',
}

export function mapScalarType(dmmfType: string): string {
  return SCALAR_TYPE_MAP[dmmfType] ?? 'unknown'
}
```

### 6. `src/build-model-interface.ts`

Generates the TypeScript interface and enum declarations.

**Field inclusion rules:**
- Include `kind === 'scalar'` and `kind === 'enum'` fields only
- Skip `kind === 'object'` (relations) and `kind === 'unsupported'`
- Optional if `!field.isRequired` → append `?` and ` | null`
- List fields → append `[]`

Also exports `buildEnumDeclarations(enums)` for the shared `enums.ts` output file.

### 7. `src/build-input-types.ts`

Generates three input types per model.

**`ModelWhereUniqueInput`:** Only fields where `field.isId === true` or `field.isUnique === true`, all optional. (Composite `uniqueIndexes` deferred to a follow-up — add a comment noting the limitation.)

**`ModelCreateInput`:** All scalar/enum fields. A field is optional when `!field.isRequired || field.hasDefaultValue || field.isUpdatedAt`. This correctly handles `@id @default(autoincrement())` (has default → optional) and `@updatedAt` (managed by server → optional).

**`ModelUpdateInput`:** All scalar/enum fields optional. Exclude `field.isReadOnly === true` (these are FK backing fields whose writes are proxied through relation objects).

### 8. `src/build-model-client.ts`

Generates a class `ModelRestClient` with five methods using native `fetch`. The resource path is the model name lowercased (`User` → `user`). A `buildQuery` helper converts plain objects to URL query strings; it is emitted inline in each model file.

Methods:
- `findMany(params?: { where?: Partial<Model>; skip?: number; take?: number }): Promise<Model[]>` — `GET /user?...`
- `findUnique(params: { where: ModelWhereUniqueInput }): Promise<Model | null>` — `GET /user/findUnique?...`, returns `null` on 404
- `create(params: { data: ModelCreateInput }): Promise<Model>` — `POST /user`
- `update(params: { where: ModelWhereUniqueInput; data: ModelUpdateInput }): Promise<Model>` — `PATCH /user?...`
- `delete(params: { where: ModelWhereUniqueInput }): Promise<Model>` — `DELETE /user?...`

### 9. `src/build-index-file.ts`

Generates the `index.ts` for the output directory. Exports `./enums` (if any) and one export per model file (using kebab-cased filenames).

### 10. `src/generate.ts`

Orchestrator. Receives `DMMF.Document` and `outputDir`:
1. `fs.mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true })`
2. For each model: produce a single `.ts` file containing interface + input types + client class (joined by double newlines), keyed by `toKebabCase(model.name) + '.ts'`
3. Write `enums.ts` from `buildEnumDeclarations`
4. Write `index.ts` from `buildIndexFile`
5. Write all files in parallel with `Promise.all`

Helper `toKebabCase`: replaces each uppercase letter (except the first) with `-` + lowercase.

### 11. `src/index.ts`

Entry point. Calls `generatorHandler` (JSON-RPC entry from `@prisma/generator-helper`):

```typescript
import type { GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
import { generatorHandler } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import { generate } from './generate'

generatorHandler({
  onManifest() {
    return {
      defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client',
      prettyName: 'Prisma REST Client',
    }
  },

  async onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = options.generator.output?.value
    if (!outputDir) {
      throw new Error(
        'An output path is required. Add `output = "./generated/rest-client"` to your generator block.',
      )
    }
    await generate(options.dmmf, outputDir)
  },
})
```

### 12. Modify `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`

Add to the `paths` section (alongside the existing entries):

```json
"@prisma/generator-rest-client": ["./packages/generator-rest-client/src"]
```

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Reason |
|---|---|
| `@prisma/generator-helper` in `devDependencies` | `getProjectExternals` in `build.ts` marks `dependencies` as external; devDeps get bundled so the generator is self-contained |
| Out-of-process (`generatorHandler`), not registered in `client-generator-registry` | External/community-style generator invoked via `node ./dist/index.js`; no in-process registry needed |
| One output file per model | Follows "avoid barrel files" coding convention; enables tree-shaking of the generated client |
| `fetch`, no axios/node-fetch | No added runtime dependency; native in Node 18+ and all browsers |
| `Json` → `unknown` | Avoids Prisma runtime type dependency (`JsonValue`) in the generated REST client |
| Composite `uniqueIndexes` deferred | Needs union-of-object types; out of scope for v1; document in a comment |
| `isReadOnly` excluded from `UpdateInput` | FK backing fields (e.g., `authorId`) are `isReadOnly: true`; direct write via relation object, not the field |

---

## Verification

```bash
# 1. Install workspace deps
pnpm install

# 2. Build the package
pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client build
# Expected: dist/index.js, dist/index.mjs, dist/index.d.ts, dist/index.d.mts

# 3. Smoke test with a real schema
# Create tests/schema.prisma (see below) and run:
cd packages/generator-rest-client
npx prisma generate --schema=tests/schema.prisma
# Expected: tests/generated/index.ts, enums.ts, user.ts, post.ts

# 4. Typecheck the bundle
tsc --project tsconfig.build.bundle.json --noEmit

# 5. Run unit tests (after adding tests/generate.test.ts)
pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client test
```

**Minimal test schema (`tests/schema.prisma`):**
```prisma
datasource db {
  provider = "sqlite"
}

generator restClient {
  provider = "node ../../dist/index.js"
  output   = "./generated"
}

enum Role { USER ADMIN }

model User {
  id    Int     @id @default(autoincrement())
  email String  @unique
  name  String?
  role  Role    @default(USER)
  posts Post[]
}

model Post {
  id       Int     @id @default(autoincrement())
  title    String
  authorId Int
  author   User    @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])
}
```

**Key vitest assertions:**
- `User` interface has `id`, `email`, `name?`, `role` — no `posts` (relation excluded)
- `UserCreateInput` has `id?` (has default), `email` (required, no default), `name?` (optional)
- `UserWhereUniqueInput` has `id?` and `email?` (both unique/id)
- `PostUpdateInput` has no `authorId` (`isReadOnly: true`)
- `enums.ts` contains `export enum Role { USER = 'USER', ADMIN = 'ADMIN' }`

| `src/build-model-client.ts` | Generates `ModelRestClient` class with 5 CRUD methods using native `fetch` |


- **One output file per model** — follows the "avoid barrel files" convention.
- **Native `fetch`** — no extra runtime dependencies in the generated client.
- **Composite unique indexes deferred** — single-field `@id`/`@unique` only in v1.

`adapterConfig` (from `helpers/compile/configs.ts`) reads `src/index.ts` and emits dual CJS/ESM bundles to `dist/`.

### 5. `src/map-scalar-type.ts`

Maps DMMF scalar type strings to TypeScript types. All types are idiomatic native TS — no Prisma runtime dependencies in the generated client.

```typescript
const SCALAR_TYPE_MAP: Record<string, string> = {
  String: 'string',
  Int: 'number',
  Float: 'number',
  Boolean: 'boolean',
  BigInt: 'bigint',
  DateTime: 'Date',
  Json: 'unknown',
  Bytes: 'string',    // base64 over REST
  Decimal: 'number',
}

export function mapScalarType(dmmfType: string): string {
  return SCALAR_TYPE_MAP[dmmfType] ?? 'unknown'
}
```

### 6. `src/build-model-interface.ts`

Generates the TypeScript interface and enum declarations.

**Field inclusion rules:**
- Include `kind === 'scalar'` and `kind === 'enum'` fields only
- Skip `kind === 'object'` (relations) and `kind === 'unsupported'`
- Optional if `!field.isRequired` → append `?` and ` | null`
- List fields → append `[]`

Also exports `buildEnumDeclarations(enums)` for the shared `enums.ts` output file.

### 7. `src/build-input-types.ts`

Generates three input types per model.

**`ModelWhereUniqueInput`:** Only fields where `field.isId === true` or `field.isUnique === true`, all optional. (Composite `uniqueIndexes` deferred to a follow-up — add a comment noting the limitation.)

**`ModelCreateInput`:** All scalar/enum fields. A field is optional when `!field.isRequired || field.hasDefaultValue || field.isUpdatedAt`. This correctly handles `@id @default(autoincrement())` (has default → optional) and `@updatedAt` (managed by server → optional).

**`ModelUpdateInput`:** All scalar/enum fields optional. Exclude `field.isReadOnly === true` (these are FK backing fields whose writes are proxied through relation objects).

### 8. `src/build-model-client.ts`

Generates a class `ModelRestClient` with five methods using native `fetch`. The resource path is the model name lowercased (`User` → `user`). A `buildQuery` helper converts plain objects to URL query strings; it is emitted inline in each model file.

Methods:
- `findMany(params?: { where?: Partial<Model>; skip?: number; take?: number }): Promise<Model[]>` — `GET /user?...`
- `findUnique(params: { where: ModelWhereUniqueInput }): Promise<Model | null>` — `GET /user/findUnique?...`, returns `null` on 404
- `create(params: { data: ModelCreateInput }): Promise<Model>` — `POST /user`
- `update(params: { where: ModelWhereUniqueInput; data: ModelUpdateInput }): Promise<Model>` — `PATCH /user?...`
- `delete(params: { where: ModelWhereUniqueInput }): Promise<Model>` — `DELETE /user?...`

### 9. `src/build-index-file.ts`

Generates the `index.ts` for the output directory. Exports `./enums` (if any) and one export per model file (using kebab-cased filenames).

### 10. `src/generate.ts`

Orchestrator. Receives `DMMF.Document` and `outputDir`:
1. `fs.mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true })`
2. For each model: produce a single `.ts` file containing interface + input types + client class (joined by double newlines), keyed by `toKebabCase(model.name) + '.ts'`
3. Write `enums.ts` from `buildEnumDeclarations`
4. Write `index.ts` from `buildIndexFile`
5. Write all files in parallel with `Promise.all`

Helper `toKebabCase`: replaces each uppercase letter (except the first) with `-` + lowercase.

### 11. `src/index.ts`

Entry point. Calls `generatorHandler` (JSON-RPC entry from `@prisma/generator-helper`):

```typescript
import type { GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
import { generatorHandler } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import { generate } from './generate'

generatorHandler({
  onManifest() {
    return {
      defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client',
      prettyName: 'Prisma REST Client',
    }
  },

  async onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = options.generator.output?.value
    if (!outputDir) {
      throw new Error(
        'An output path is required. Add `output = "./generated/rest-client"` to your generator block.',
      )
    }
    await generate(options.dmmf, outputDir)
  },
})
```

### 12. Modify `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`

Add to the `paths` section (alongside the existing entries):

```json
"@prisma/generator-rest-client": ["./packages/generator-rest-client/src"]
```

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Reason |
|---|---|
| `@prisma/generator-helper` in `devDependencies` | `getProjectExternals` in `build.ts` marks `dependencies` as external; devDeps get bundled so the generator is self-contained |
| Out-of-process (`generatorHandler`), not registered in `client-generator-registry` | External/community-style generator invoked via `node ./dist/index.js`; no in-process registry needed |
| One output file per model | Follows "avoid barrel files" coding convention; enables tree-shaking of the generated client |
| `fetch`, no axios/node-fetch | No added runtime dependency; native in Node 18+ and all browsers |
| `Json` → `unknown` | Avoids Prisma runtime type dependency (`JsonValue`) in the generated REST client |
| Composite `uniqueIndexes` deferred | Needs union-of-object types; out of scope for v1; document in a comment |
| `isReadOnly` excluded from `UpdateInput` | FK backing fields (e.g., `authorId`) are `isReadOnly: true`; direct write via relation object, not the field |

---

## Verification

```bash
# 1. Install workspace deps
pnpm install

# 2. Build the package
pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client build
# Expected: dist/index.js, dist/index.mjs, dist/index.d.ts, dist/index.d.mts

# 3. Smoke test with a real schema
# Create tests/schema.prisma (see below) and run:
cd packages/generator-rest-client
npx prisma generate --schema=tests/schema.prisma
# Expected: tests/generated/index.ts, enums.ts, user.ts, post.ts

# 4. Typecheck the bundle
tsc --project tsconfig.build.bundle.json --noEmit

# 5. Run unit tests (after adding tests/generate.test.ts)
pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client test
```

**Minimal test schema (`tests/schema.prisma`):**
```prisma
datasource db {
  provider = "sqlite"
}

generator restClient {
  provider = "node ../../dist/index.js"
  output   = "./generated"
}

enum Role { USER ADMIN }

model User {
  id    Int     @id @default(autoincrement())
  email String  @unique
  name  String?
  role  Role    @default(USER)
  posts Post[]
}

model Post {
  id       Int     @id @default(autoincrement())
  title    String
  authorId Int
  author   User    @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])
}
```

**Key vitest assertions:**
- `User` interface has `id`, `email`, `name?`, `role` — no `posts` (relation excluded)
- `UserCreateInput` has `id?` (has default), `email` (required, no default), `name?` (optional)
- `UserWhereUniqueInput` has `id?` and `email?` (both unique/id)
- `PostUpdateInput` has no `authorId` (`isReadOnly: true`)
- `enums.ts` contains `export enum Role { USER = 'USER', ADMIN = 'ADMIN' }`

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: `@prisma/generator-rest-client`

## Context

Add a new Prisma generator package to the monorepo that generates a typed TypeScript REST API client from a Prisma schema. The generator implements the standard Prisma out-of-process generator protocol (JSON-RPC over stdin/stderr via `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper`). For each model it produces: a TypeScript interface, three input types (`WhereUniqueInput`, `CreateInput`, `UpdateInput`), and a `RestClient` class with `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete` methods that use the native `fetch` API.

---

## Critical Files to Create

```
packages/generator-rest-client/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.build.json
├── helpers/build.ts
└── src/
    ├── index.ts               ← generatorHandler entry point
    ├── generate.ts            ← orchestrates output file writes
    ├── map-scalar-type.ts     ← DMMF scalar type → TS type string
    ├── build-model-interface.ts
    ├── build-input-types.ts
    ├── build-model-client.ts
    └── build-index-file.ts
```

## Critical File to Modify

- `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` — add path alias for the new package

---

## Implementation Steps

### 1. `package.json`

Follow the pattern of `packages/client-generator-ts/package.json`.

**Key constraint (confirmed from `helpers/compile/build.ts` `getProjectExternals`):** When `bundle: true`, everything in `dependencies` and `peerDependencies` is marked **external** (not bundled). Since the generator must be a self-contained executable (`node ./dist/index.js`), `@prisma/generator-helper` and `@prisma/dmmf` go in `devDependencies` so they are bundled into the output.

```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/generator-rest-client",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "Prisma generator that produces a typed REST API client",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": { "types": "./dist/index.d.ts", "default": "./dist/index.js" },
      "import": { "types": "./dist/index.d.mts", "default": "./dist/index.mjs" }
    }
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/generator-rest-client"
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "dependencies": {},
  "devDependencies": {
    "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator-helper": "workspace:*"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

### 2. `tsconfig.json`

```json
{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.json" }
```

### 3. `tsconfig.build.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "declaration" },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

### 4. `helpers/build.ts`

```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(adapterConfig)
```

`adapterConfig` (from `helpers/compile/configs.ts`) reads `src/index.ts` and emits dual CJS/ESM bundles to `dist/`.

### 5. `src/map-scalar-type.ts`

Maps DMMF scalar type strings to TypeScript types. All types are idiomatic native TS — no Prisma runtime dependencies in the generated client.

```typescript
const SCALAR_TYPE_MAP: Record<string, string> = {
  String: 'string',
  Int: 'number',
  Float: 'number',
  Boolean: 'boolean',
  BigInt: 'bigint',
  DateTime: 'Date',
  Json: 'unknown',
  Bytes: 'string',    // base64 over REST
  Decimal: 'number',
}

export function mapScalarType(dmmfType: string): string {
  return SCALAR_TYPE_MAP[dmmfType] ?? 'unknown'
}
```

### 6. `src/build-model-interface.ts`

Generates the TypeScript interface and enum declarations.

**Field inclusion rules:**
- Include `kind === 'scalar'` and `kind === 'enum'` fields only
- Skip `kind === 'object'` (relations) and `kind === 'unsupported'`
- Optional if `!field.isRequired` → append `?` and ` | null`
- List fields → append `[]`

Also exports `buildEnumDeclarations(enums)` for the shared `enums.ts` output file.

### 7. `src/build-input-types.ts`

Generates three input types per model.

**`ModelWhereUniqueInput`:** Only fields where `field.isId === true` or `field.isUnique === true`, all optional. (Composite `uniqueIndexes` deferred to a follow-up — add a comment noting the limitation.)

**`ModelCreateInput`:** All scalar/enum fields. A field is optional when `!field.isRequired || field.hasDefaultValue || field.isUpdatedAt`. This correctly handles `@id @default(autoincrement())` (has default → optional) and `@updatedAt` (managed by server → optional).

**`ModelUpdateInput`:** All scalar/enum fields optional. Exclude `field.isReadOnly === true` (these are FK backing fields whose writes are proxied through relation objects).

### 8. `src/build-model-client.ts`

Generates a class `ModelRestClient` with five methods using native `fetch`. The resource path is the model name lowercased (`User` → `user`). A `buildQuery` helper converts plain objects to URL query strings; it is emitted inline in each model file.

Methods:
- `findMany(params?: { where?: Partial<Model>; skip?: number; take?: number }): Promise<Model[]>` — `GET /user?...`
- `findUnique(params: { where: ModelWhereUniqueInput }): Promise<Model | null>` — `GET /user/findUnique?...`, returns `null` on 404
- `create(params: { data: ModelCreateInput }): Promise<Model>` — `POST /user`
- `update(params: { where: ModelWhereUniqueInput; data: ModelUpdateInput }): Promise<Model>` — `PATCH /user?...`
- `delete(params: { where: ModelWhereUniqueInput }): Promise<Model>` — `DELETE /user?...`

### 9. `src/build-index-file.ts`

Generates the `index.ts` for the output directory. Exports `./enums` (if any) and one export per model file (using kebab-cased filenames).

### 10. `src/generate.ts`

Orchestrator. Receives `DMMF.Document` and `outputDir`:
1. `fs.mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true })`
2. For each model: produce a single `.ts` file containing interface + input types + client class (joined by double newlines), keyed by `toKebabCase(model.name) + '.ts'`
3. Write `enums.ts` from `buildEnumDeclarations`
4. Write `index.ts` from `buildIndexFile`
5. Write all files in parallel with `Promise.all`

Helper `toKebabCase`: replaces each uppercase letter (except the first) with `-` + lowercase.

### 11. `src/index.ts`

Entry point. Calls `generatorHandler` (JSON-RPC entry from `@prisma/generator-helper`):

```typescript
import type { GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
import { generatorHandler } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import { generate } from './generate'

generatorHandler({
  onManifest() {
    return {
      defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client',
      prettyName: 'Prisma REST Client',
    }
  },

  async onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = options.generator.output?.value
    if (!outputDir) {
      throw new Error(
        'An output path is required. Add `output = "./generated/rest-client"` to your generator block.',
      )
    }
    await generate(options.dmmf, outputDir)
  },
})
```

### 12. Modify `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`

Add to the `paths` section (alongside the existing entries):

```json
"@prisma/generator-rest-client": ["./packages/generator-rest-client/src"]
```

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Reason |
|---|---|
| `@prisma/generator-helper` in `devDependencies` | `getProjectExternals` in `build.ts` marks `dependencies` as external; devDeps get bundled so the generator is self-contained |
| Out-of-process (`generatorHandler`), not registered in `client-generator-registry` | External/community-style generator invoked via `node ./dist/index.js`; no in-process registry needed |
| One output file per model | Follows "avoid barrel files" coding convention; enables tree-shaking of the generated client |
| `fetch`, no axios/node-fetch | No added runtime dependency; native in Node 18+ and all browsers |
| `Json` → `unknown` | Avoids Prisma runtime type dependency (`JsonValue`) in the generated REST client |
| Composite `uniqueIndexes` deferred | Needs union-of-object types; out of scope for v1; document in a comment |
| `isReadOnly` excluded from `UpdateInput` | FK backing fields (e.g., `authorId`) are `isReadOnly: true`; direct write via relation object, not the field |

---

## Verification

```bash
# 1. Install workspace deps
pnpm install

# 2. Build the package
pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client build
# Expected: dist/index.js, dist/index.mjs, dist/index.d.ts, dist/index.d.mts

# 3. Smoke test with a real schema
# Create tests/schema.prisma (see below) and run:
cd packages/generator-rest-client
npx prisma generate --schema=tests/schema.prisma
# Expected: tests/generated/index.ts, enums.ts, user.ts, post.ts

# 4. Typecheck the bundle
tsc --project tsconfig.build.bundle.json --noEmit

# 5. Run unit tests (after adding tests/generate.test.ts)
pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client test
```

**Minimal test schema (`tests/schema.prisma`):**
```prisma
datasource db {
  provider = "sqlite"
}

generator restClient {
  provider = "node ../../dist/index.js"
  output   = "./generated"
}

enum Role { USER ADMIN }

model User {
  id    Int     @id @default(autoincrement())
  email String  @unique
  name  String?
  role  Role    @default(USER)
  posts Post[]
}

model Post {
  id       Int     @id @default(autoincrement())
  title    String
  authorId Int
  author   User    @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])
}
```

**Key vitest assertions:**
- `User` interface has `id`, `email`, `name?`, `role` — no `posts` (relation excluded)
- `UserCreateInput` has `id?` (has default), `email` (required, no default), `name?` (optional)
- `UserWhereUniqueInput` has `id?` and `email?` (both unique/id)
- `PostUpdateInput` has no `authorId` (`isReadOnly: true`)
- `enums.ts` contains `export enum Role { USER = 'USER', ADMIN = 'ADMIN' }`

29	
30	- **`devDependencies` for bundled deps** — directly confirmed from `helpers/compile/build.ts` `getProjectExternals()`: when `bundle: true`, all `dependencies` are marked external by esbuild. Putting `@prisma/generator-helper` and `@prisma/dmmf` in `devDependencies` means they get bundled into `dist/index.js`, making the generator self-contained.
31	- **Out-of-process `generatorHandler`** — no changes to `client-generator-registry`. Users invoke it via `provider = "node ./dist/index.js"` in their schema.

file.content:
Here's the implementation plan for `@prisma/generator-rest-client`:

---

## What will be created

**10 new files** in `packages/generator-rest-client/`:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `package.json` | `@prisma/generator-helper` & `@prisma/dmmf` in **`devDependencies`** so they bundle into the self-contained executable |
| `tsconfig.json` / `tsconfig.build.json` | Extends existing root configs |
| `helpers/build.ts` | 2-line `adapterConfig` build → dual CJS/ESM to `dist/` |
| `src/index.ts` | `generatorHandler` entry point — `onManifest` + `onGenerate` |
| `src/generate.ts` | Iterates models, writes one `.ts` file per model + `enums.ts` + `index.ts` |
| `src/map-scalar-type.ts` | `String→string`, `DateTime→Date`, `Json→unknown`, `Bytes→string`, etc. |
| `src/build-model-interface.ts` | `interface Model {}` — scalar/enum fields only; relations excluded |
| `src/build-input-types.ts` | `WhereUniqueInput` (id/unique fields), `CreateInput` (auto-defaults optional), `UpdateInput` (all optional, no `isReadOnly` FK fields) |
| `src/build-model-client.ts` | `ModelRestClient` class with 5 CRUD methods using native `fetch` |
| `src/build-index-file.ts` | Output `index.ts` with `export * from './model-name'` per model |

**1 existing file modified:**

- **`tsconfig.build.bundle.json`** — add `"@prisma/generator-rest-client": ["./packages/generator-rest-client/src"]` to `paths`

---

## Key confirmed design decisions

- **`devDependencies` for bundled deps** — directly confirmed from `helpers/compile/build.ts` `getProjectExternals()`: when `bundle: true`, all `dependencies` are marked external by esbuild. Putting `@prisma/generator-helper` and `@prisma/dmmf` in `devDependencies` means they get bundled into `dist/index.js`, making the generator self-contained.
- **Out-of-process `generatorHandler`** — no changes to `client-generator-registry`. Users invoke it via `provider = "node ./dist/index.js"` in their schema.
- **One file per model** — follows the monorepo's "avoid barrel files" convention.
- **Native `fetch`** — no extra runtime deps in the generated client.
- **Composite `uniqueIndexes` deferred** — v1 handles only single-field `@id`/`@unique`; documented with a comment.

1	import { ChokidarOptions, watch as createWatcher } from 'chokidar'
2	import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
3	import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
4	import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
5	import glob from 'globby'
6	import path from 'path'
7	
8	import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
9	import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
10	import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
11	import { map } from '../blaze/map'
12	import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
13	import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
14	import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
15	import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
16	import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
17	import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
18	import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'
19	
20	export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
21	export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
22	  name?: string
23	  emitTypes?: boolean
24	  emitMetafile?: boolean
25	  outbase?: never // we don't support this
26	}
27	
28	const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
29	  platform: 'node',
30	  target: 'ES2022',
31	  logLevel: 'error',
32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
33	  metafile: true,
34	} as const
35	
36	/**
37	 * Apply defaults to the original build options
38	 * @param options the original build options
39	 */
40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}
233

4	
5	type AdapterEntry = {
6	  entry: string
7	  outfile: string
8	}
9	
10	export function createAdapterConfig(entries: AdapterEntry[]): BuildOptions[] {
11	  return entries.flatMap(({ entry, outfile }) => {
12	    const baseName = path.basename(outfile)
13	
14	    return [
15	      {
16	        name: `${baseName}-cjs`,
17	        format: 'cjs' as const,
18	        bundle: true,
19	        entryPoints: [entry],
20	        outfile,
21	        outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
22	        emitTypes: true,
23	      },
24	      {
25	        name: `${baseName}-esm`,
26	        format: 'esm' as const,
27	        bundle: true,
28	        entryPoints: [entry],
29	        outfile,
30	        outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
31	        emitTypes: true,
32	      },
33	    ]
34	  })
35	}
36	
37	export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])
38	
39	export const unbundledConfig: BuildOptions[] = [
40	  {
41	    name: 'cjs',
42	    format: 'cjs',
43	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
44	    outdir: 'dist',
45	    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
46	    emitTypes: true,
47	  },
48	  {
49	    name: 'esm',
50	    format: 'esm',
51	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
52	    outdir: 'dist',
53	    outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
54	    emitTypes: true,
55	  },
56	]
57

import { ReadonlyDeep } from './util'

export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}>

export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}>

export type OtherOperationMappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  read: string[]
  write: string[]
}>

export type DatamodelEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]
  dbName?: string | null
  documentation?: string
}>

export type SchemaEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: string[]
}>

export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
}>

export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}>

export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: string[]
}>
export type PrimaryKey = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string | null
  fields: string[]
}>
export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}>

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'
export type FieldLocation = 'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'

export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  /**
   * Describes the data type in the same the way it is defined in the Prisma schema:
   * BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
   */
  type: string
  /**
   * Native database type, if specified.
   * For example, `@db.VarChar(191)` is encoded as `['VarChar', ['191']]`,
   * `@db.Text` is encoded as `['Text', []]`.
   */
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}>

export type FieldDefault = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: Array<string | number>
}>

export type FieldDefaultScalar = string | boolean | number

export type Index = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  type: IndexType
  isDefinedOnField: boolean
  name?: string
  dbName?: string
  algorithm?: string
  clustered?: boolean
  fields: IndexField[]
}>

export type IndexType = 'id' | 'normal' | 'unique' | 'fulltext'

export type IndexField = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  sortOrder?: SortOrder
  length?: number
  operatorClass?: string
}>

export type SortOrder = 'asc' | 'desc'

export type Schema = ReadonlyDeep<{
  rootQueryType?: string
  rootMutationType?: string
  inputObjectTypes: {
    // For now there are no `model` InputTypes
    model?: InputType[]
    prisma?: InputType[]
  }
  outputObjectTypes: {
    model: OutputType[]
    prisma: OutputType[]
  }
  enumTypes: {
    model?: SchemaEnum[]
    prisma: SchemaEnum[]
  }
  fieldRefTypes: {
    prisma?: FieldRefType[]
  }
}>

export type Query = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: SchemaArg[]
  output: QueryOutput
}>

export type QueryOutput = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
}>

export type TypeRef<AllowedLocations extends FieldLocation> = {
  isList: boolean
  type: string
  location: AllowedLocations
  namespace?: FieldNamespace
}

export type InputTypeRef = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'>

export type SchemaArg = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  comment?: string
  isNullable: boolean
  isRequired: boolean
  inputTypes: InputTypeRef[]
  isParameterizable: boolean
  requiresOtherFields?: string[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
}>

export type OutputType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: SchemaField[]
}>

export type SchemaField = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  isNullable?: boolean
  outputType: OutputTypeRef
  args: SchemaArg[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
  documentation?: string
}>

export type OutputTypeRef = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes'>

export type Deprecation = ReadonlyDeep<{
  sinceVersion: string
  reason: string
  plannedRemovalVersion?: string
}>

export type InputType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  constraints: {
    maxNumFields: number | null
    minNumFields: number | null
    fields?: string[]
  }
  meta?: {
    source?: string
    grouping?: string
  }
  fields: SchemaArg[]
}>

export type FieldRefType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  allowTypes: FieldRefAllowType[]
  fields: SchemaArg[]
}>

export type FieldRefAllowType = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'enumTypes'>

export type ModelMapping = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  plural: string
  findUnique?: string | null
  findUniqueOrThrow?: string | null
  findFirst?: string | null
  findFirstOrThrow?: string | null
  findMany?: string | null
  create?: string | null
  createMany?: string | null
  createManyAndReturn?: string | null
  update?: string | null
  updateMany?: string | null
  updateManyAndReturn?: string | null
  upsert?: string | null
  delete?: string | null
  deleteMany?: string | null
  aggregate?: string | null
  groupBy?: string | null
  count?: string | null
  findRaw?: string | null
  aggregateRaw?: string | null
}>

export enum ModelAction {
  findUnique = 'findUnique',
  findUniqueOrThrow = 'findUniqueOrThrow',
  findFirst = 'findFirst',
  findFirstOrThrow = 'findFirstOrThrow',
  findMany = 'findMany',
  create = 'create',
  createMany = 'createMany',
  createManyAndReturn = 'createManyAndReturn',
  update = 'update',
  updateMany = 'updateMany',
  updateManyAndReturn = 'updateManyAndReturn',
  upsert = 'upsert',
  delete = 'delete',
  deleteMany = 'deleteMany',
  groupBy = 'groupBy',
  count = 'count', // TODO: count does not actually exist in DMMF
  aggregate = 'aggregate',
  findRaw = 'findRaw',
  aggregateRaw = 'aggregateRaw',
}

I also need to update `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`. Let me work through the implementation for each file, starting with the utility modules that handle type mapping and model generation.

For `package.json`, I need to add `@prisma/generator-helper` and `@prisma/dmmf` as dev dependencies so they get bundled, and set up the `"bin"` entry point for the CLI.

For the build setup, I'm thinking about how to handle this generator. Since it runs as a Node.js process, I should create a bundled CommonJS executable with a shebang rather than dual CJS/ESM outputs. Let me check the build approach—I'll use the `adapterConfig` from the helpers to handle the bundling properly.

Actually, looking at the plan again, `adapterConfig` produces both CJS and ESM outputs to `dist/`. For a generator binary, the `package.json` `bin` field should point to the CJS version at `dist/index.js`. The generator handler reads from stdin, so it doesn't need a shebang since the framework invokes it with Node directly.

Setting up the package.json with the main entry, types, and dev dependencies that get bundled...

Now I'm mapping out the utility modules: scalar type conversion, model interface generation, and input type builders that will handle the schema transformation.

Then there's the model client generator that creates the REST client class with fetch-based CRUD operations, an index file exporter for each model, the main generator orchestration logic, and finally the entry point with the generator handler.

Writing the implementation files... I'm mapping Prisma scalar types to their TypeScript equivalents, handling enums separately and converting types like String to string, Int/Float to number, BigInt to bigint, DateTime to Date, and Json/Bytes/Decimal to their appropriate representations. Now I'm thinking through how this utility gets used across the different builder modules, particularly in the model interface and input type generation, so I'll keep the implementation straightforward and focused on the type mapping logic. Building input type generators... Building the REST client class... Writing the CRUD methods... I'm building the index file by mapping each model name to a kebab-case export statement, then adding the enums export at the end. I also need a helper function to convert camelCase names to kebab-case by handling both lowercase-to-uppercase and uppercase sequence transitions.

Now I'm setting up the generator file with imports for all the builder functions that handle creating index files, input types, model clients, and model interfaces. I'm also defining the kebab-case conversion utility again, then starting to construct the enums file by checking if there are any enums to process and mapping each one to generate its member definitions. I'm setting up the file generation pipeline—creating the enums file, then iterating through each model to write individual TypeScript files with their interfaces and client code, and finally generating an index file that exports everything.

import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

/**
 * Maps a Prisma field to its corresponding TypeScript type string.
 * For enum fields the enum name is returned directly.
 */
export function mapScalarType(field: Pick<DMMF.Field, 'kind' | 'type'>): string {
  if (field.kind === 'enum') return field.type

  switch (field.type) {
    case 'String':
      return 'string'
    case 'Int':
    case 'Float':
      return 'number'
    case 'BigInt':
      return 'bigint'
    case 'Boolean':
      return 'boolean'
    case 'DateTime':
      return 'Date'
    case 'Json':
      return 'unknown'
    case 'Bytes':
      // Represented as base64-encoded strings over HTTP.
      return 'string'
    case 'Decimal':
      // Represented as strings to preserve precision over JSON.
      return 'string'
    default:
      return 'unknown'
  }
}

import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

import { mapScalarType } from './map-scalar-type'

/**
 * Generates three input-type interfaces for a model:
 *
 * - `WhereUniqueInput` — holds only `@id` and `@unique` fields, all optional
 *   so callers can pass whichever unique key they have. Composite unique
 *   indexes (multiple fields) are not handled in v1.
 * - `CreateInput` — all scalar/enum fields; fields that carry a server-side
 *   default (`hasDefaultValue`, `isUpdatedAt`) are made optional.
 * - `UpdateInput` — same fields as CreateInput but all optional; read-only FK
 *   shadow fields (`isReadOnly`) are excluded.
 */
export function buildInputTypes(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const scalarOrEnum = model.fields.filter((f) => f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum')

  const whereUniqueLines = scalarOrEnum
    .filter((f) => f.isId || f.isUnique)
    .map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${mapScalarType(f)}`)

  const createLines = scalarOrEnum.map((f) => {
    const tsType = mapScalarType(f)
    const list = f.isList ? '[]' : ''
    const optional = !f.isRequired || f.hasDefaultValue || f.isUpdatedAt ? '?' : ''
    return `  ${f.name}${optional}: ${tsType}${list}`
  })

  const updateLines = scalarOrEnum
    .filter((f) => !f.isReadOnly)
    .map((f) => {
      const tsType = mapScalarType(f)
      const list = f.isList ? '[]' : ''
      return `  ${f.name}?: ${tsType}${list}`
    })

  return [
    `export interface ${model.name}WhereUniqueInput {\n${whereUniqueLines.join('\n')}\n}`,
    `export interface ${model.name}CreateInput {\n${createLines.join('\n')}\n}`,
    `export interface ${model.name}UpdateInput {\n${updateLines.join('\n')}\n}`,
  ].join('\n\n')
}

import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

/**
 * Generates the `{Model}RestClient` class for a model.
 *
 * The class provides five CRUD methods backed by the native `fetch` API:
 * `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`. URL conventions
 * follow REST best practices: collection at `/{plural}`, filtering through
 * query-string parameters derived from `WhereUniqueInput`.
 */
export function buildModelClient(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const name = model.name
  const plural = toPlural(name)

  const lines = [
    `export class ${name}RestClient {`,
    `  #baseUrl: string`,
    ``,
    `  constructor(baseUrl: string) {`,
    `    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl.replace(/\\/$/, '')`,
    `  }`,
    ``,
    `  async findMany(): Promise<${name}[]> {`,
    `    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${plural}\`)`,
    `    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(\`GET /${plural} failed: \${res.status}\`)`,
    `    return res.json() as Promise<${name}[]>`,
    `  }`,
    ``,
    `  async findUnique(where: ${name}WhereUniqueInput): Promise<${name} | null> {`,
    `    const params = new URLSearchParams(where as Record<string, string>)`,
    `    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${plural}?\${params}\`)`,
    `    if (res.status === 404) return null`,
    `    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(\`GET /${plural} failed: \${res.status}\`)`,
    `    return res.json() as Promise<${name}>`,
    `  }`,
    ``,
    `  async create(data: ${name}CreateInput): Promise<${name}> {`,
    `    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${plural}\`, {`,
    `      method: 'POST',`,
    `      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },`,
    `      body: JSON.stringify(data),`,
    `    })`,
    `    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(\`POST /${plural} failed: \${res.status}\`)`,
    `    return res.json() as Promise<${name}>`,
    `  }`,
    ``,
    `  async update(where: ${name}WhereUniqueInput, data: ${name}UpdateInput): Promise<${name}> {`,
    `    const params = new URLSearchParams(where as Record<string, string>)`,
    `    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${plural}?\${params}\`, {`,
    `      method: 'PATCH',`,
    `      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },`,
    `      body: JSON.stringify(data),`,
    `    })`,
    `    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(\`PATCH /${plural} failed: \${res.status}\`)`,
    `    return res.json() as Promise<${name}>`,
    `  }`,
    ``,
    `  async delete(where: ${name}WhereUniqueInput): Promise<${name}> {`,
    `    const params = new URLSearchParams(where as Record<string, string>)`,
    `    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${plural}?\${params}\`, { method: 'DELETE' })`,
    `    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(\`DELETE /${plural} failed: \${res.status}\`)`,
    `    return res.json() as Promise<${name}>`,
    `  }`,
    `}`,
  ]

  return lines.join('\n')
}

function toPlural(name: string): string {
  const lower = name.toLowerCase()
  if (lower.endsWith('y') && !/[aeiou]y$/.test(lower)) {
    return lower.slice(0, -1) + 'ies'
  }
  if (lower.endsWith('s') || lower.endsWith('x') || lower.endsWith('z') || lower.endsWith('ch') || lower.endsWith('sh')) {
    return lower + 'es'
  }
  return lower + 's'
}

    `    const params = new URLSearchParams(where as Record<string, string>)`,
    `    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${plural}?\${params}\`, { method: 'DELETE' })`,
    `    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(\`DELETE /${plural} failed: \${res.status}\`)`,
    `    return res.json() as Promise<${name}>`,
    `  }`,
    `}`,
  ]

  return lines.join('\n')
}

function toPlural(name: string): string {
  const lower = name.toLowerCase()
  if (lower.endsWith('y') && !/[aeiou]y$/.test(lower)) {
    return lower.slice(0, -1) + 'ies'
  }
  if (lower.endsWith('s') || lower.endsWith('x') || lower.endsWith('z') || lower.endsWith('ch') || lower.endsWith('sh')) {
    return lower + 'es'
  }
  return lower + 's'
}
